column1: # K.2b aggregation manifest — labels each matmul-v4-report JSON with the two
# decision inputs the JSON itself does not carry: {vendor, class}. Consumed by
# contrib/matmul-v4/k2b-gate.py via --manifest.
# Format: tab-separated. One row per measured box/backend.
#   <host-or-file>   <vendor>   <class>   [<part>]
# - <host-or-file> matches the report's `host` field, its filename, or a
#   substring of either (so `b200` matches matmul-v4-report-b200-cuda.json).
# - <vendor>  free text; the cross-vendor count (spec §9 item 1) groups by it.
#             Use DISTINCT vendors — two NVIDIA parts do not satisfy ">=2 vendors".
# - <class>   one of: datacenter | consumer | apple | other
#             (backend==cpu is auto-classified cpu-ref and ignored here.)
# - <part>    optional human label for the printout.
# Replace the illustrative rows below with your real fleet. Lines starting with
# '#' and blank lines are ignored.
# host-or-file        vendor    class        part
b200                  nvidia    datacenter   B200
b300                  nvidia    datacenter   B300/GB300
mi355                 amd       datacenter   MI355X
rtx5090               nvidia    consumer     RTX5090
m5max                 apple     apple        M5Max
# trainium3           aws       datacenter   Trainium3   # once a Kind::Trainium backend lands
# tpuv7               google    datacenter   TPUv7       # once a Kind::TPU backend lands
